since_last: 37% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +2.53%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 37% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +0.02% · BTC -2.77% · Gold +1.35% · Nasdaq100 +0.83%. 37% of 87 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +2.53%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +2.53%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -3.13%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +17.10%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +17.10%, AMAT +10.86%, TER +8.81%, KLAC +7.90%, QCOM +6.85%. Laggards: INOD -11.02%, PLTR -5.74%, DELL -5.70%, AI -5.22%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -1.29% at $152.55
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -1.29% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
